newsSinceJun6: Stock jumped +7.1% on June 9 following the May 2026 monthly revenue release (~+43.65% YoY), confirming AI data-center power/cooling demand acceleration. This follows fresh Vera Rubin-era product news: a June 3 prefabricated modular AI data center launch (deployment time cut ~60%), a 2.4MW liquid-to-liquid CDU, and a new cold plate for NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72. Q1 results (revenue +36% YoY, operating profit +102%, record 37% gross margin) were already known. The move is fundamental in origin but confirms a consensus thesis on a stock now +142.7% YTD at ~$199B market cap.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance quote 2308.TW (price action, May revenue +43.65% YoY)
Digitimes - Delta prefabricated modular AI data center launch (Jun 3, 2026)
Startup Fortune - Delta: AI data centers need power as much as chips (Rubin NVL72 cold plate, 2.4MW CDU)
Investing.com - Delta Q1 2026 beats with 101% profit surge
BigGo Finance - Delta Q1 FY2026 earnings call: record 37% gross margin, EPS NT$7.91

verdicts.rankRationale: On a crowding-sensitive alpha list, a +7.1% single-day pop on a widely watched monthly revenue print extends the rerating without revealing anything hidden; at +142.7% YTD and ~$199B cap, residual under-owned upside shrinks further even though the news is fundamentally good.
verdicts.bull: May revenue +43.65% YoY plus Vera Rubin NVL72 cold-plate and 2.4MW CDU content show Delta's rack-power/cooling dollar content is still compounding into the Rubin cycle.
verdicts.neutral: Real fundamental confirmation arrived, but the market repriced it same-day; the evidence and the rerating roughly cancel for residual alpha purposes.
verdicts.bear: A $199B mega-cap up 142.7% YTD on a consensus AI-power narrative is the definition of crowded; any pause in hyperscaler capex or power-shelf pricing pressure triggers sharp multiple compression.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued monthly revenue acceleration and Rubin-generation power/cooling design-win disclosures.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: multiple compression on any AI capex digestion despite intact fundamentals.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold: rank 91 already encodes that Delta is no longer a latent node, and today's headline-grabbing +7.1% pop makes it even less undiscovered, but the May revenue print is genuine fundamental confirmation of the Power & Components linkage that offsets the latency erosion.
verdicts.bull: Delta keeps deepening data-center power and liquid-cooling content (Rubin NVL72 cold plate, modular AI data centers), keeping it structurally central to the power node.
verdicts.neutral: Fully discovered name executing well; on a latency-weighted list it neither deserves promotion nor a forced demotion from an already-low rank.
verdicts.bear: Zero discovery premium left; monthly revenue prints are now front-page events, so the latent-node thesis is exhausted and only beta to the AI-power theme remains.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Liquid-cooling and modular data center order momentum into the Rubin ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Name is fully consensus; underperforms genuinely latent power suppliers on any rotation.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold after yesterday's down move: the May revenue acceleration (+43.65% YoY) is direct new evidence on the exact execution watch-item in the thesis (power supplies/thermal management), but the +7.1% day and +142.7% YTD rerating consume that evidence on a list balancing bottleneck scarcity against recognition.
verdicts.bear: At ~$199B and post-rerating, Delta trades as a recognized AI infrastructure proxy; further upside requires sustained beats, and cyclicality in power electronics cuts both ways.
verdicts.bull: Execution is verifiably on track: record 37% gross margin, May revenue +43.65% YoY, and rising per-rack content (power shelves, Rubin cold plates, 2.4MW CDUs) into the Rubin generation.
verdicts.neutral: Strong fundamentals already moved the rank down for recognition reasons yesterday; today's print validates the thesis without changing the scarcity-vs-recognition balance.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June/Q2 revenue prints sustaining 40%+ YoY growth and Rubin-era power/cooling content wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Pricing pressure or share loss in AI power shelves as competitors (Lite-On, Flex, Vertiv ecosystem) chase the same sockets.
